title: Splunk ITSI Maintenance Window Compliance, Service-Suppression Drift, Notable-Event Suppression Coverage Audits, and Out-of-Window Change Forensics
exclusions: Does not replace UC-13.2.1 service health trending, UC-13.2.2 KPI degradation alerting, UC-13.2.3 episode volume analytics, UC-13.2.5 KPI base search performance, UC-13.2.6 correlation rules fidelity, UC-13.2.7 predictive analytics, UC-13.2.8 glass table reliability, UC-13.2.9 Elasticsearch ingest monitoring, or UC-13.2.18 adaptive threshold calibration. Does not auto-close maintenance objects; it produces evidence for human change governance.
evidence: Saved search uc_13_2_13_itsi_maintenance_window_governance_multisearch on a fifteen-minute schedule; weekly CSV exports joined to itsi_service_inventory.csv and cmdb_change_window_inventory.csv; REST export hashes for itsi_maintenance_calendar.json and itsi_notable_event_suppression.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab ITSI stack, ingest internal_maint_calendar_lane rows where end_time is before now while maintenance_status remains active, pair with a standard change_request_id missing the serviceid in cmdb_change_window_inventory.csv, and confirm uc_13_2_13_itsi_maintenance_window_governance_multisearch returns critical severity with non-null recommended_action within thirty minutes.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After closing the maintenance object, aligning CMDB allowlists, and clearing test notables, filtering severity to critical and high should return zero rows for the lab serviceid across four consecutive schedules.

dataSources: index=itsi_summary for serviceid, kpiid, kpi_name, entity_alias, is_in_maintenance, alert_level, maintenance linkage; index=itsi_tracked_alerts for notable_event_id, severity, maintenance_window_id; index=_internal sourcetype=itsi_maintenance_calendar for REST-forwarded itsi_maintenance_calendar.json style snapshots with maintenance_id, maintenance_status, start_time, end_time; index=servicenow sourcetype=snow:change_request for change_request_id, change_state, planned_start, planned_end, actual_start, actual_end; periodic REST exports of itsi_notable_event_suppression definitions mirrored into operations indexes; lookups itsi_service_inventory.csv and cmdb_change_window_inventory.csv
spl:
`comment("UC-13.2.13 ITSI maintenance-window governance audit: itsi_summary KPI lane, itsi_tracked_alerts notables, _internal itsi_maintenance_calendar, ServiceNow change_request, rolling maintenance share baseline via tstats. earliest=-7d@d latest=now")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=itsi_summary earliest=-7d@d latest=now
      | eval serviceid=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,itsi_service_id,service_id,"")))
      | eval service_name=trim(toString(coalesce(service_name,service_title,itsi_service_name,"")))
      | eval kpiid=trim(toString(coalesce(kpiid,kpi_id,"")))
      | eval kpi_name=trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_name,kpi,"")))
      | eval entity_alias=trim(toString(coalesce(entity_alias,entity_key,entity_identifier,"")))
      | eval maintenance_id=trim(toString(coalesce(maintenance_id,maintenance_window_id,mw_id,"")))
      | eval maintenance_status=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(alert_level,maintenance_status,"kpi_summary_observed"))))
      | eval start_time=_time
      | eval end_time=null()
      | eval severity=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(severity,"info"))))
      | eval notable_event_id=""
      | eval is_in_maintenance=if(match(lower(trim(toString(coalesce(in_maintenance_kpi,is_in_maintenance,kpi_in_maint,"0")))),"true|1|yes|suppressed|under_maint"),1,0)
      | eval change_request_id=upper(trim(toString(coalesce(change_request_id,chg_ticket,""))))
      | eval change_state=""
      | eval planned_start=null()
      | eval planned_end=null()
      | eval actual_start=null()
      | eval actual_end=null()
      | eval lane="itsi_summary_kpi_lane" ]
    [ search index=itsi_tracked_alerts earliest=-7d@d latest=now
      | eval serviceid=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,service_id,itsi_service_id,"")))
      | eval service_name=trim(toString(coalesce(service_name,itsi_service_name,"")))
      | eval kpiid=trim(toString(coalesce(kpiid,kpi_id,"")))
      | eval kpi_name=trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_name,"")))
      | eval entity_alias=trim(toString(coalesce(entity_alias,entity_name,entity_title,"")))
      | eval maintenance_id=trim(toString(coalesce(maintenance_window_id,maintenance_id,"")))
      | eval maintenance_status=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(maintenance_status,"notable_tracked"))))
      | eval start_time=_time
      | eval end_time=null()
      | eval severity=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(severity,itsi_severity,"medium"))))
      | eval notable_event_id=trim(toString(coalesce(notable_event_id,event_id,itsi_notable_id,"")))
      | eval is_in_maintenance=if(len(maintenance_id)>0 OR match(lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tracked_in_mw_flag,"")))),"true|1|yes"),1,0)
      | eval change_request_id=upper(trim(toString(coalesce(change_request_id,external_ticket,""))))
      | eval change_state=""
      | eval planned_start=null()
      | eval planned_end=null()
      | eval actual_start=null()
      | eval actual_end=null()
      | eval lane="itsi_tracked_notables_lane" ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=itsi_maintenance_calendar earliest=-7d@d latest=now
      | eval serviceid=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,service_id,scoped_service_id,"")))
      | eval service_name=trim(toString(coalesce(service_name,service_title,"maint_calendar_object")))
      | eval kpiid=trim(toString(coalesce(kpiid,"")))
      | eval kpi_name=trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_name,"")))
      | eval entity_alias=trim(toString(coalesce(entity_alias,entity_scope,scope_token,"")))
      | eval maintenance_id=trim(toString(coalesce(maintenance_id,mw_id,object_id,"")))
      | eval maintenance_status=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(maintenance_status,status,state,log_action,"logged"))))
      | eval stp=strptime(trim(toString(coalesce(start_time,start_time_iso,""))),"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%Z")
      | eval stp=if(isnull(stp),strptime(trim(toString(coalesce(start_time,""))),"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"),stp)
      | eval etp=strptime(trim(toString(coalesce(end_time,end_time_iso,""))),"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%Z")
      | eval etp=if(isnull(etp),strptime(trim(toString(coalesce(end_time,""))),"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"),etp)
      | eval start_time=coalesce(stp,_time)
      | eval end_time=etp
      | eval severity="info"
      | eval notable_event_id=""
      | eval is_in_maintenance=if(match(maintenance_status,"active|open|scheduled|enabled|in_progress"),1,0)
      | eval change_request_id=upper(trim(toString(coalesce(change_ticket,chg_ref,external_ref,""))))
      | eval change_state=""
      | eval planned_start=start_time
      | eval planned_end=end_time
      | eval actual_start=null()
      | eval actual_end=null()
      | eval lane="internal_maint_calendar_lane" ]
    [ search index=servicenow sourcetype=snow:change_request earliest=-7d@d latest=now
      | eval serviceid=trim(toString(coalesce(business_service,cmdb_ci_service,u_business_service,"")))
      | eval service_name=trim(toString(coalesce(u_service_name,short_description,"change_record")))
      | eval kpiid=""
      | eval kpi_name=""
      | eval entity_alias=trim(toString(coalesce(cmdb_ci,u_cmdb_ci,"")))
      | eval maintenance_id=""
      | eval maintenance_status=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(state,u_state,stage,"workflow"))))
      | eval pst=strptime(trim(toString(coalesce(planned_start,work_start,start_date,u_planned_start,""))),"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
      | eval pet=strptime(trim(toString(coalesce(planned_end,work_end,end_date,u_planned_end,""))),"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
      | eval ast=strptime(trim(toString(coalesce(actual_start,u_actual_start,""))),"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
      | eval aet=strptime(trim(toString(coalesce(actual_end,u_actual_end,""))),"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
      | eval start_time=coalesce(pst,_time)
      | eval end_time=pet
      | eval severity="info"
      | eval notable_event_id=""
      | eval is_in_maintenance=0
      | eval change_request_id=upper(trim(toString(coalesce(number,u_number,task_id,chg_number,""))))
      | eval change_state=maintenance_status
      | eval planned_start=pst
      | eval planned_end=pet
      | eval actual_start=ast
      | eval actual_end=aet
      | eval lane="servicenow_change_lane" ]
| eval serviceid=if(len(serviceid)>0,serviceid,"unscoped_service")
| join type=left max=1 serviceid
    [| inputlookup itsi_service_inventory.csv
     | eval serviceid=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,service_id,"")))
     | eval svc_owner_team=trim(toString(coalesce(owner_team,custodian_team,team,"")))
     | eval svc_tier=trim(toString(coalesce(criticality_tier,service_tier,"")))
     | fields serviceid svc_owner_team svc_tier parent_service_id ]
| join type=left max=1 change_request_id
    [| inputlookup cmdb_change_window_inventory.csv
     | eval change_request_id=upper(trim(toString(coalesce(change_request_id,chg_number,task_ref,""))))
     | eval approved_service_set=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(approved_services_csv,service_allowlist,ci_allowlist,""))))
     | eval window_planned_start=strptime(trim(toString(coalesce(planned_start,chg_planned_start,""))),"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
     | eval window_planned_end=strptime(trim(toString(coalesce(planned_end,chg_planned_end,""))),"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
     | eval change_type_risk=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(change_type,risk_class,chg_model,"standard"))))
     | fields change_request_id approved_service_set window_planned_start window_planned_end change_type_risk ]
| eval service_name=coalesce(service_name,"unknown_service")
| eval svc_owner_team=coalesce(svc_owner_team,"unassigned_owner")
| eval now_epoch=now()
| eval overdue_active=if(match(lane,"internal_maint_calendar_lane") AND is_in_maintenance==1 AND isnum(end_time) AND end_time>0 AND end_time<now_epoch,1,0)
| eval svc_low=lower(serviceid)
| eval rogue_scope=if(match(lane,"internal_maint_calendar_lane") AND len(change_request_id)>2 AND len(approved_service_set)>2 AND !isnull(svc_low) AND !match(approved_service_set,svc_low) AND !match(change_type_risk,"emergency|expedited"),1,0)
| eval leak_notable=if(match(lane,"itsi_tracked_notables_lane") AND len(notable_event_id)>2 AND len(maintenance_id)==0 AND match(severity,"high|critical|major|error"),1,0)
| eval missing_maint_hint=if(match(lane,"servicenow_change_lane") AND len(change_request_id)>2 AND isnotnull(planned_start) AND isnotnull(planned_end) AND match(change_state,"implement|work_in_progress|scheduled"),1,0)
| eval post_window_health_drop=0
| sort 0 serviceid _time
| streamstats window=64 current=t global=f count AS svc_seq sum(eval(if(is_in_maintenance==1,1,0))) AS maint_streak BY serviceid
| eventstats count AS evt_den sum(eval(if(is_in_maintenance==1,1,0))) AS evt_maint_num max(eval(if(match(lane,"internal_maint_calendar_lane"),is_in_maintenance,0))) AS cal_maint_peak BY serviceid
| eval maint_pct_window=if(evt_den>0,round(100*evt_maint_num/evt_den,2),0)
| eval maint_abuse=if(maint_pct_window>20 AND cal_maint_peak>0,1,0)
| join max=1 serviceid
    [| tstats count AS summary_hist_pts WHERE index=itsi_summary earliest=-30d@d latest=-8d@d BY serviceid ]
| eval cascade_risk=if(match(lane,"itsi_tracked_notables_lane") AND len(parent_service_id)>0 AND len(maintenance_id)==0 AND match(severity,"medium|high|critical"),1,0)
| eval severity=case(
    overdue_active==1 OR rogue_scope==1,"critical",
    leak_notable==1 OR maint_abuse==1,"high",
    cascade_risk==1 OR maint_pct_window>15,"medium",
    missing_maint_hint==1,"low",
    true(),"info")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    overdue_active==1,"close_or_split_stale_maintenance_window_and_refresh_itsi_maintenance_calendar_json_snapshot",
    rogue_scope==1,"reconcile_scope_with_cmdb_change_window_inventory_or_attach_emergency_record",
    leak_notable==1,"expand_notable_event_suppression_rules_review_itsi_notable_event_suppression_rest_export",
    maint_abuse==1,"govern_per_team_maintenance_dwell_cap_and_executive_review_chronic_parking",
    cascade_risk==1,"align_child_kpi_entity_aliases_under_parent_service_tree_maintenance_scope",
    missing_maint_hint==1,"confirm_operator_placed_matching_service_into_maintenance_or_correct_false_change_schedule",
    true(),"refresh_multisearch_lanes_and_verify_indexer_time_sync")
| table serviceid service_name kpiid kpi_name entity_alias maintenance_id maintenance_status start_time end_time severity notable_event_id is_in_maintenance change_request_id change_state planned_start planned_end actual_start actual_end overdue_active rogue_scope maint_pct_window leak_notable recommended_action svc_owner_team lane summary_hist_pts
description: Detects Splunk ITSI maintenance calendar governance failures by correlating index=itsi_summary KPI maintenance posture, index=itsi_tracked_alerts notable leakage, index=_internal maintenance calendar audit shims, and ServiceNow change_request schedules against CMDB-approved service inventories: overdue windows past end_time, rogue suppression outside approved change scope, missing maintenance for scheduled changes, high rolling maintenance dwell that leaves services effectively unmonitored, cascade coverage gaps between parent and child entities, and suppression rule drift versus REST-exported itsi_notable_event_suppression content.
value: Platform and audit stakeholders receive a defensible ledger that ties planned downtime objects to change records, quantifies how often services sit in maintenance, explains why notables still fired during quiet periods, and highlights teams that chronically park services in suppression so executives can intervene before customer-impacting outages hide inside policy gaps.
implementation: Schedule uc_13_2_13_itsi_maintenance_window_governance_multisearch every fifteen minutes with earliest=-7d@d; land index=itsi_summary, index=itsi_tracked_alerts, index=_internal sourcetype=itsi_maintenance_calendar, and index=servicenow sourcetype=snow:change_request; maintain itsi_service_inventory.csv and cmdb_change_window_inventory.csv; tune maint_pct_window thresholds with change governance; page on critical or high; archive weekly CSV exports.
visualization: Severity-colored table by serviceid and maintenance_id with drilldowns to raw itsi_summary points, itsi_tracked_alerts rows, _internal maintenance JSON, snow:change_request payloads, lookup-enriched owner teams, and timeline overlays for maint_pct_window versus summary_hist_pts.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=f count FROM datamodel=Change WHERE nodename=Change Change.action="modified" earliest=-7d@d latest=now BY Change.user Change.object | rename Change.user AS change_user Change.object AS change_object | where like(lower(change_object),"%maintenance%") OR like(lower(change_user),"%itsi%") | table change_user change_object count
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Splunk CIM Change summaries when ITSI maintenance mutations or ServiceNow updates map into Change.cmd, Change.user, and Change.object; retain thirty days for quarterly compliance attestations.

references.title: Splunk Docs — Schedule maintenance downtime in ITSI
Splunk Docs — Overview of maintenance windows in ITSI
Splunk Docs — Overview of services in ITSI
Splunk Docs — ITSI summary index reference
Splunk Docs — Overview of Event Analytics in ITSI
Splunk Lantern — Moving to observability with ITSI
Splunk Lantern — Working with event analytics in ITSI
Axelos ITIL — Change management practice guidance

knownFalsePositives: Deliberately long maintenance windows for known multi-day migrations can present as overdue_active until operators split work into sequential tickets; require human review against the implementation plan before paging. Emergency change records opened post-hoc may lack cmdb_change_window_inventory.csv rows at the moment maintenance starts; honor change_type_risk and defer rogue_scope until the CMDB snapshot catches up. Maintenance calendar time zones versus Splunk indexer UTC can create false overdue impressions when end_time is stored as local civil time without offset; reconcile in the ITSI UI. Demoted services intentionally parked in maintenance for cost-saving observability reductions should be documented in risk registers so maint_abuse findings route to the correct executive sponsor rather than the on-call engineer. Scheduled holiday change freezes sometimes stack overlapping maintenance_id values that look like collisions but reflect intentional quiet periods; compare to the enterprise freeze calendar. Intentional overrun for documented risk acceptance should carry a linked problem record so auditors understand why end_time exceeded planned_end. Partner-managed services may legitimately suppress alerts without internal change numbers; use lookup notes to avoid false rogue_scope. Validation notables fired immediately after maintenance can resemble leak_notable until suppression rules intentionally allow test traffic; read change tasks before escalating. Content-pack upgrades that bulk-refresh maintenance objects may spike internal_maint_calendar_lane volume without misconduct; filter known automation actors. tstats baseline drift after index migrations can empty summary_hist_pts; repair acceleration before interpreting historical context. Jira-sourced changes mapped into ServiceNow mirrors can duplicate planned_start; deduplicate change_request_id keys in cmdb_change_window_inventory.csv to prevent noisy missing_maint_hint rows.

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: required — maintenance calendar, tracked alerts, service context, summary indexes

premiumApps.name: Splunk Enterprise Security
premiumApps.note: optional — risk-notable correlation when governance breaches route to SOC workflows

detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with Splunk IT Service Intelligence administrators who curate maintenance calendar objects and suppression policies, change-management process owners who interpret ServiceNow or Jira change records as the authoritative schedule, audit partners who need tamper-resistant evidence of planned suppression, and network operations leaders who rely on maintenance windows to keep paging honest during approved work. This use case isolates maintenance window governance: the planned-suppression membrane that hides KPI-driven alerts and notable events while change activity is authorized. Detection axis (a) flags maintenance intervals whose recorded end_time is already before the current clock yet maintenance_status still behaves as if the window were active, which often means an operator forgot to close the object or an automation job stalled after a long migration. Axis (b) compares services, KPI identifiers, and entity_alias values that appear inside active maintenance against cmdb_change_window_inventory.csv and broader configuration sources so you can spot rogue suppression where a team parks production in quiet mode without an approved change line item, potentially masking genuine outages. Axis (c) walks the opposite direction: change_request_id rows with planned_start and planned_end from ServiceNow where the matching service_name never registered is_in_maintenance in the ITSI lane, explaining alert noise that change teams blame on monitoring when the real gap was forgetting to open the calendar. Axis (d) measures per-service maintenance percentage across the rolling search window so chronic parking above roughly twenty percent surfaces as a compliance finding because the service is effectively unmonitored for a material slice of the month. Axis (e) reserves space in your deployment for post-window KPI or service health score regression logic by persisting pre-window and post-window slices from index=itsi_summary into summary or analytic indexes; the exemplar SPL seeds post_window_health_drop for you to wire to saved searches that compare one hour before versus one hour after each maintenance_id closes. Axis (f) highlights notable_event_id rows in index=itsi_tracked_alerts that arrive while maintenance should suppress them, using leak_notable when linkage fields are empty despite high severity. Axis (g) encodes cascade_risk hints when parent_service_id from itsi_service_inventory.csv suggests a child entity is still firing while the parent tree should have inherited suppression coverage. Axis (h) uses svc_owner_team to spotlight teams whose services accumulate maint_abuse or overdue_active rows for executive conversations about operational discipline. Axis (i) distinguishes standard versus emergency change_type_risk values from the CMDB snapshot so expedited work is not misclassified as rogue_scope when the allowlist legitimately diverges. Axis (j) expects periodic REST inventory exports of itsi_maintenance_calendar.json and itsi_notable_event_suppression into either index=_internal with sourcetype=itsi_maintenance_calendar or a dedicated operations index so this search can replay the same objects auditors see in the REST response without relying on the browser alone.

Scope boundaries stay crisp so reviewers do not duplicate sibling gold use cases. UC-13.2.1 service health trending answers how scores move over time, not whether downtime objects were legitimate. UC-13.2.2 KPI degradation alerting focuses on threshold breaches, not maintenance policy. UC-13.2.3 episode volume and mean time analytics measure responder workflow, not calendar hygiene. UC-13.2.5 base search performance economics stay separate from governance. UC-13.2.6 correlation rules fidelity is not the same as suppression coverage. UC-13.2.7 predictive analytics does not replace compliance forensics. UC-13.2.8 glass table reliability is visualization plane work. UC-13.2.9 Elasticsearch ingest pipeline monitoring is unrelated. UC-13.2.18 adaptive threshold tuning is explicitly out of scope here. Splunk Enterprise Security notables may still correlate with maintenance breaches, but this UC remains anchored on ITSI maintenance calendars and CMDB alignment rather than generic security analytics.

RBAC and safety: grant search access to index=itsi_summary, index=itsi_tracked_alerts, index=servicenow, and index=_internal maintenance feeds only to roles that already handle operational telemetry; segregate KV store write capabilities from analysts who merely need read-only multisearch results. Service accounts that snapshot REST exports must rotate credentials and never reuse personal passwords. Change records can include customer identifiers; mask or hash fields per privacy policy before landing in shared indexes. When you enrich with Jira change payloads, apply the same data minimization guidance as ServiceNow.

Index and retention design: keep at least thirty days hot for itsi_summary and itsi_tracked_alerts, ninety days warm for maintenance audit replicas, and align _internal sampling awareness with your platform policy because verbose maintenance logging can grow quickly. If _internal volume is too constrained, mirror sourcetype=itsi_maintenance_calendar events into index=itsi_ops_governance with higher retention. Stamp cluster_id or search_head_cluster on every forwarded JSON snapshot when multiple ITSI stacks exist.

Field normalization: insist that forwarders emit serviceid, service_name, kpiid, kpi_name, entity_alias, maintenance_id, maintenance_status, start_time, end_time, severity, notable_event_id, is_in_maintenance, change_request_id, change_state, planned_start, planned_end, actual_start, and actual_end even when upstream REST uses camelCase or dotted paths. Use props.conf FIELDALIAS and EVAL to collapse duplicates so multisearch arms align without fragile rename chains at search time.

Risk briefing: maintenance governance searches are sensitive politically because they accuse teams of hiding outages or neglecting change discipline; pair quantitative findings with change advisory notes before escalating. Emergency change processes legitimately open shorter windows with imperfect CMDB coverage; tune rogue_scope to honor change_type_risk. Time zone offsets between ServiceNow, Splunk indexers, and ITSI UI users can make end_time appear overdue when the clock is simply expressed in UTC versus local civil time; always verify against the maintenance user interface before paging.

Differentiation recap: maintenance calendar compliance, suppression drift, CMDB alignment, rolling maintenance percentage abuse, cascade coverage hints, REST inventory replay, and notable leak forensics — not service health storytelling, not KPI quality scoring, not threshold tuning.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up four multisearch lanes plus supporting snapshots so Splunk can fuse KPI evidence, notables, internal maintenance logs, and authoritative change payloads without assuming a single sourcetype is complete.

Lane one — index=itsi_summary: accelerate or at least normalize summary metrics that include serviceid, kpiid, and hints about whether KPI samples were recorded under maintenance. Schedule summary index backups if you rely on tstats for historical baselines. Document which alert_level or health score fields you will average for post-window regression extensions tied to axis (e).

Lane two — index=itsi_tracked_alerts: confirm notables retain notable_event_id, service linkage, maintenance_window_id when ITSI associates the event with a window, and severity text suitable for leak_notable logic. If your pipeline strips linkage, fix transforms before trusting suppression analytics.

Lane three — index=_internal sourcetype=itsi_maintenance_calendar: implement a modular input or saved search that hits the maintenance calendar REST export your team approves, writing one event per maintenance object with canonical JSON in _raw. Include action verbs when the logging shim records create, extend, or delete operations. When Splunk Cloud limits _internal retention, duplicate the same feed into a dedicated governance index.

Lane four — index=servicenow sourcetype=snow:change_request: normalize change_request_id, planned_start, planned_end, actual_start, actual_end, change_state, and CI identifiers so they join cleanly to cmdb_change_window_inventory.csv. Align timestamps to UTC at ingestion.

Lookup maintenance: refresh itsi_service_inventory.csv weekly with serviceid, service_name, svc_owner_team, svc_tier, and parent_service_id for cascade analytics. Refresh cmdb_change_window_inventory.csv with change_request_id, approved_service_set, window_planned_start, window_planned_end, and change_type_risk classifications. For Jira change exports, mirror the same columns into a parallel lookup if ServiceNow is not your system of record.

REST inventory discipline: schedule read-only exports that write itsi_maintenance_calendar.json and itsi_notable_event_suppression definitions into versioned object storage quarterly, then ingest compact excerpts into Splunk for diffing. Hash payloads when auditors require tamper evidence.

props.conf guidance: TRUNCATE generously for wide JSON, TIME_PREFIX and TIME_FORMAT tuned to ISO-8601 maintenance timestamps, and LINE_BREAKER rules that tolerate batched arrays from REST.

Pre-save validation: each lane must return events within one hour after enablement; run controlled maintenance in lab to produce a known maintenance_id and confirm all four arms surface it.

Dashboards: host a governance dashboard with severity-colored tables, drilldowns to raw JSON for each lane, and deep links to change tickets.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as uc_13_2_13_itsi_maintenance_window_governance_multisearch with a fifteen minute schedule during steady-state production, dispatch earliest=-7d@d, dispatch latest=now, and alert when severity is critical or high. Throttle duplicate serviceid, maintenance_id pairs for forty-five minutes unless severity escalates. Include recommended_action text in pager descriptions so incident commanders open the correct runbook without re-deriving eval logic.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans index=itsi_summary KPI observations, index=itsi_tracked_alerts notables, index=_internal maintenance calendar shims, and index=servicenow change_request rows so one silent lane cannot hide a governance gap. coalesce() ladders absorb exporter-specific field names. join binds itsi_service_inventory.csv and cmdb_change_window_inventory.csv for owner and allowlist context. streamstats tracks short rolling maintenance streak counters per serviceid while eventstats computes maint_pct_window density hints. A tstats join against index=itsi_summary supplies summary_hist_pts historical counts for volume context. case() maps overdue_active, rogue_scope, leak_notable, maint_abuse, cascade_risk, and missing_maint_hint into severities with recommended_action routing.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.2.13 ITSI maintenance-window governance audit: itsi_summary KPI lane, itsi_tracked_alerts notables, _internal itsi_maintenance_calendar, ServiceNow change_request, rolling maintenance share baseline via tstats. earliest=-7d@d latest=now")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=itsi_summary earliest=-7d@d latest=now
      | eval serviceid=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,itsi_service_id,service_id,"")))
      | eval service_name=trim(toString(coalesce(service_name,service_title,itsi_service_name,"")))
      | eval kpiid=trim(toString(coalesce(kpiid,kpi_id,"")))
      | eval kpi_name=trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_name,kpi,"")))
      | eval entity_alias=trim(toString(coalesce(entity_alias,entity_key,entity_identifier,"")))
      | eval maintenance_id=trim(toString(coalesce(maintenance_id,maintenance_window_id,mw_id,"")))
      | eval maintenance_status=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(alert_level,maintenance_status,"kpi_summary_observed"))))
      | eval start_time=_time
      | eval end_time=null()
      | eval severity=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(severity,"info"))))
      | eval notable_event_id=""
      | eval is_in_maintenance=if(match(lower(trim(toString(coalesce(in_maintenance_kpi,is_in_maintenance,kpi_in_maint,"0")))),"true|1|yes|suppressed|under_maint"),1,0)
      | eval change_request_id=upper(trim(toString(coalesce(change_request_id,chg_ticket,""))))
      | eval change_state=""
      | eval planned_start=null()
      | eval planned_end=null()
      | eval actual_start=null()
      | eval actual_end=null()
      | eval lane="itsi_summary_kpi_lane" ]
    [ search index=itsi_tracked_alerts earliest=-7d@d latest=now
      | eval serviceid=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,service_id,itsi_service_id,"")))
      | eval service_name=trim(toString(coalesce(service_name,itsi_service_name,"")))
      | eval kpiid=trim(toString(coalesce(kpiid,kpi_id,"")))
      | eval kpi_name=trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_name,"")))
      | eval entity_alias=trim(toString(coalesce(entity_alias,entity_name,entity_title,"")))
      | eval maintenance_id=trim(toString(coalesce(maintenance_window_id,maintenance_id,"")))
      | eval maintenance_status=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(maintenance_status,"notable_tracked"))))
      | eval start_time=_time
      | eval end_time=null()
      | eval severity=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(severity,itsi_severity,"medium"))))
      | eval notable_event_id=trim(toString(coalesce(notable_event_id,event_id,itsi_notable_id,"")))
      | eval is_in_maintenance=if(len(maintenance_id)>0 OR match(lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tracked_in_mw_flag,"")))),"true|1|yes"),1,0)
      | eval change_request_id=upper(trim(toString(coalesce(change_request_id,external_ticket,""))))
      | eval change_state=""
      | eval planned_start=null()
      | eval planned_end=null()
      | eval actual_start=null()
      | eval actual_end=null()
      | eval lane="itsi_tracked_notables_lane" ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=itsi_maintenance_calendar earliest=-7d@d latest=now
      | eval serviceid=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,service_id,scoped_service_id,"")))
      | eval service_name=trim(toString(coalesce(service_name,service_title,"maint_calendar_object")))
      | eval kpiid=trim(toString(coalesce(kpiid,"")))
      | eval kpi_name=trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_name,"")))
      | eval entity_alias=trim(toString(coalesce(entity_alias,entity_scope,scope_token,"")))
      | eval maintenance_id=trim(toString(coalesce(maintenance_id,mw_id,object_id,"")))
      | eval maintenance_status=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(maintenance_status,status,state,log_action,"logged"))))
      | eval stp=strptime(trim(toString(coalesce(start_time,start_time_iso,""))),"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%Z")
      | eval stp=if(isnull(stp),strptime(trim(toString(coalesce(start_time,""))),"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"),stp)
      | eval etp=strptime(trim(toString(coalesce(end_time,end_time_iso,""))),"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%Z")
      | eval etp=if(isnull(etp),strptime(trim(toString(coalesce(end_time,""))),"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"),etp)
      | eval start_time=coalesce(stp,_time)
      | eval end_time=etp
      | eval severity="info"
      | eval notable_event_id=""
      | eval is_in_maintenance=if(match(maintenance_status,"active|open|scheduled|enabled|in_progress"),1,0)
      | eval change_request_id=upper(trim(toString(coalesce(change_ticket,chg_ref,external_ref,""))))
      | eval change_state=""
      | eval planned_start=start_time
      | eval planned_end=end_time
      | eval actual_start=null()
      | eval actual_end=null()
      | eval lane="internal_maint_calendar_lane" ]
    [ search index=servicenow sourcetype=snow:change_request earliest=-7d@d latest=now
      | eval serviceid=trim(toString(coalesce(business_service,cmdb_ci_service,u_business_service,"")))
      | eval service_name=trim(toString(coalesce(u_service_name,short_description,"change_record")))
      | eval kpiid=""
      | eval kpi_name=""
      | eval entity_alias=trim(toString(coalesce(cmdb_ci,u_cmdb_ci,"")))
      | eval maintenance_id=""
      | eval maintenance_status=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(state,u_state,stage,"workflow"))))
      | eval pst=strptime(trim(toString(coalesce(planned_start,work_start,start_date,u_planned_start,""))),"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
      | eval pet=strptime(trim(toString(coalesce(planned_end,work_end,end_date,u_planned_end,""))),"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
      | eval ast=strptime(trim(toString(coalesce(actual_start,u_actual_start,""))),"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
      | eval aet=strptime(trim(toString(coalesce(actual_end,u_actual_end,""))),"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
      | eval start_time=coalesce(pst,_time)
      | eval end_time=pet
      | eval severity="info"
      | eval notable_event_id=""
      | eval is_in_maintenance=0
      | eval change_request_id=upper(trim(toString(coalesce(number,u_number,task_id,chg_number,""))))
      | eval change_state=maintenance_status
      | eval planned_start=pst
      | eval planned_end=pet
      | eval actual_start=ast
      | eval actual_end=aet
      | eval lane="servicenow_change_lane" ]
| eval serviceid=if(len(serviceid)>0,serviceid,"unscoped_service")
| join type=left max=1 serviceid
    [| inputlookup itsi_service_inventory.csv
     | eval serviceid=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,service_id,"")))
     | eval svc_owner_team=trim(toString(coalesce(owner_team,custodian_team,team,"")))
     | eval svc_tier=trim(toString(coalesce(criticality_tier,service_tier,"")))
     | fields serviceid svc_owner_team svc_tier parent_service_id ]
| join type=left max=1 change_request_id
    [| inputlookup cmdb_change_window_inventory.csv
     | eval change_request_id=upper(trim(toString(coalesce(change_request_id,chg_number,task_ref,""))))
     | eval approved_service_set=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(approved_services_csv,service_allowlist,ci_allowlist,""))))
     | eval window_planned_start=strptime(trim(toString(coalesce(planned_start,chg_planned_start,""))),"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
     | eval window_planned_end=strptime(trim(toString(coalesce(planned_end,chg_planned_end,""))),"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
     | eval change_type_risk=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(change_type,risk_class,chg_model,"standard"))))
     | fields change_request_id approved_service_set window_planned_start window_planned_end change_type_risk ]
| eval service_name=coalesce(service_name,"unknown_service")
| eval svc_owner_team=coalesce(svc_owner_team,"unassigned_owner")
| eval now_epoch=now()
| eval overdue_active=if(match(lane,"internal_maint_calendar_lane") AND is_in_maintenance==1 AND isnum(end_time) AND end_time>0 AND end_time<now_epoch,1,0)
| eval svc_low=lower(serviceid)
| eval rogue_scope=if(match(lane,"internal_maint_calendar_lane") AND len(change_request_id)>2 AND len(approved_service_set)>2 AND !isnull(svc_low) AND !match(approved_service_set,svc_low) AND !match(change_type_risk,"emergency|expedited"),1,0)
| eval leak_notable=if(match(lane,"itsi_tracked_notables_lane") AND len(notable_event_id)>2 AND len(maintenance_id)==0 AND match(severity,"high|critical|major|error"),1,0)
| eval missing_maint_hint=if(match(lane,"servicenow_change_lane") AND len(change_request_id)>2 AND isnotnull(planned_start) AND isnotnull(planned_end) AND match(change_state,"implement|work_in_progress|scheduled"),1,0)
| eval post_window_health_drop=0
| sort 0 serviceid _time
| streamstats window=64 current=t global=f count AS svc_seq sum(eval(if(is_in_maintenance==1,1,0))) AS maint_streak BY serviceid
| eventstats count AS evt_den sum(eval(if(is_in_maintenance==1,1,0))) AS evt_maint_num max(eval(if(match(lane,"internal_maint_calendar_lane"),is_in_maintenance,0))) AS cal_maint_peak BY serviceid
| eval maint_pct_window=if(evt_den>0,round(100*evt_maint_num/evt_den,2),0)
| eval maint_abuse=if(maint_pct_window>20 AND cal_maint_peak>0,1,0)
| join max=1 serviceid
    [| tstats count AS summary_hist_pts WHERE index=itsi_summary earliest=-30d@d latest=-8d@d BY serviceid ]
| eval cascade_risk=if(match(lane,"itsi_tracked_notables_lane") AND len(parent_service_id)>0 AND len(maintenance_id)==0 AND match(severity,"medium|high|critical"),1,0)
| eval severity=case(
    overdue_active==1 OR rogue_scope==1,"critical",
    leak_notable==1 OR maint_abuse==1,"high",
    cascade_risk==1 OR maint_pct_window>15,"medium",
    missing_maint_hint==1,"low",
    true(),"info")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    overdue_active==1,"close_or_split_stale_maintenance_window_and_refresh_itsi_maintenance_calendar_json_snapshot",
    rogue_scope==1,"reconcile_scope_with_cmdb_change_window_inventory_or_attach_emergency_record",
    leak_notable==1,"expand_notable_event_suppression_rules_review_itsi_notable_event_suppression_rest_export",
    maint_abuse==1,"govern_per_team_maintenance_dwell_cap_and_executive_review_chronic_parking",
    cascade_risk==1,"align_child_kpi_entity_aliases_under_parent_service_tree_maintenance_scope",
    missing_maint_hint==1,"confirm_operator_placed_matching_service_into_maintenance_or_correct_false_change_schedule",
    true(),"refresh_multisearch_lanes_and_verify_indexer_time_sync")
| table serviceid service_name kpiid kpi_name entity_alias maintenance_id maintenance_status start_time end_time severity notable_event_id is_in_maintenance change_request_id change_state planned_start planned_end actual_start actual_end overdue_active rogue_scope maint_pct_window leak_notable recommended_action svc_owner_team lane summary_hist_pts
```

savedsearches.conf sketch: cron every fifteen minutes, dispatch.earliest=-7d@d, dispatch.latest=now, alert when severity in critical or high after filtering info rows.

Performance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time above your objective, materialize the four lanes hourly into summary indexes keyed on serviceid and maintenance_id, widen operational alerts to earliest=-24h@h on the summaries, and retain this full search for investigations.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins in the ITSI maintenance user interface: open maintenance_id referenced by internal_maint_calendar_lane rows and compare start_time and end_time to Splunk. When timestamps disagree, verify time zone normalization and duplicate serviceid labels across stacks before blaming parsers.

Overdue validation: reproduce a lab window whose end_time is intentionally in the past while status remains active; confirm overdue_active flags critical within two schedules.

Rogue suppression validation: inject a calendar object scoped to a serviceid absent from cmdb_change_window_inventory.csv for a standard change type; confirm rogue_scope triggers and disappears when you add the service to the allowlist.

Leak validation: generate a controlled notable with high severity and empty maintenance_window_id while a maintenance window is active; confirm leak_notable populates and clears when suppression rules cover the entity_alias.

Missing maintenance validation: create a ServiceNow change with planned_start and planned_end without opening ITSI maintenance; confirm missing_maint_hint surfaces low severity until operators align processes.

Rolling percentage validation: simulate dense maintenance ticks for a non-production service and confirm maint_pct_window crosses fifteen and twenty percent thresholds predictably.

tstats validation: compare summary_hist_pts to a manual event count in index=itsi_summary for the same historical window; discrepancies indicate permissions or acceleration gaps.

Synthetic negative test: after closing lab windows and aligning CMDB rows, filtering to critical and high should return zero rows for test fixtures across four consecutive schedules.

Runbook linkage: document emergency change allowances so validators do not file false defects when change_type_risk legitimately bypasses allowlists.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — maintenance_id still active after end_time elapsed: close or split the window in ITSI, capture who extended it in audit logs, and update the ServiceNow actual_end to reflect reality.

Case 2 — rogue_scope for a standard change: reconcile cmdb_change_window_inventory.csv with the implementation plan, attach the missing CI, or downgrade only after change advisory approval documents the scope gap.

Case 3 — leak_notable storms during a declared freeze: export itsi_notable_event_suppression REST content, compare to entity_alias coverage, and widen suppression patterns cautiously with security review.

Case 4 — maint_abuse above twenty percent for a revenue service: escalate to the owning director, require executive sign-off for extended parking, and consider KPI exemptions only after risk acceptance.

Case 5 — cascade_risk firing on child KPIs: rebuild service tree membership so child entity_alias values inherit parent maintenance scope, and verify base searches reference the corrected entities.

Case 6 — missing_maint_hint with valid work but wrong clock: fix timezone transforms between ServiceNow and Splunk before accusing operators of skipping maintenance.

Case 7 — overdue_active caused by automation hang: restart the maintenance export input, verify itsi_maintenance_calendar.json snapshots resumed, and backfill gaps from REST.

Case 8 — summary_hist_pts null: repair tstats permissions on index=itsi_summary or widen earliest windows; do not mute governance alerts solely because history is empty.

Case 9 — emergency change bypass confusion: ensure change_type_risk encodes expedited workflows so rogue_scope does not page on authorized break-glass work.

Case 10 — CMDB stale while ITSI current: refresh cmdb_change_window_inventory.csv from the configuration system of record before opening audit findings.

Case 11 — post-close regression suspected: extend the saved search with dedicated one-hour pre and post tstats slices on service health fields and store results in a summary index for trend review.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists index=itsi_summary, index=itsi_tracked_alerts, index=_internal sourcetype=itsi_maintenance_calendar, and index=servicenow sourcetype=snow:change_request arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, join, inputlookup, and tstats appear; final table includes serviceid, service_name, maintenance_id, severity, notable_event_id, change_request_id, maint_pct_window, recommended_action, svc_owner_team, lane, and summary_hist_pts among analyst-visible columns.
grandmaExplanation: We check that planned quiet periods for changes match the official schedule, catch when urgent notices slip through while work is supposed to be hushed, and flag when quiet shields stay open too long or hide services without proper approval.
hardware: Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud search heads running ITSI with access to itsi_summary, itsi_tracked_alerts, _internal or dedicated governance indexes for maintenance REST shims, ServiceNow change ingestion, and CSV lookups refreshed from CMDB exports.
